spec_match.rationale: Demo exercises the core API surface — every title keyword (canvas, enhanced, textmetrics) and 3 summary keywords appear directly in the page.

interactive.rationale: Real interactive surface: 3 form controls wired to ~5450 chars of script that mutate the page state.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and concept lede share 6 keywords; the page delivers what the index promises.

edge_cases.rationale: Some defensive coding present (feature-detect or try/catch), but the happy path is dominant — explicit demos of failure modes would round it out.

references.rationale: Page links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5075532483657728 directly in the see-also section.

design_system.rationale: All colours flow through CSS variables, no raw hex, and button overrides keep colour and background in sync.

openQuestions.title: Add an explicit edge-case branch
openQuestions.detail: The page covers the happy path. Pick at least one failure mode from the spec (missing permission, empty input, unsupported value, rate-limit, etc.) and wire a button that drives the API into that branch with the resulting error / fallback shown in the page.

summary: No rubric failures remain after adding the ChromeStatus anchor; one minor edge-case follow-up remains.
